title: Paneer Shami Kebab

ingredients.title: For the Kebab Mixture
items: Chana dal (split Bengal gram) – ¼ cup (50 g), soaked 2 hrs
Black pepper – ½ tsp, freshly ground

ingredients.title: Binder
items: Besan (roasted gram flour) – 2 tbsp (roast lightly before use)

ingredients.title: For Cooking
items: Ghee + Oil (50:50 mix) – for shallow frying

instruction.title: Prepare the Base
steps: Drain soaked chana dal and pressure cook with ½ cup water, ginger, garlic, and green chilies until soft (2–3 whistles).
Cook uncovered till completely dry (no moisture).
Cool slightly, then grind into a coarse paste (not too smooth).

instruction.title: Mix the Kebab Dough
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine grated paneer, dal paste, onion, herbs, and spices.
Rest mixture for 15 minutes in the fridge for firmness.

instruction.title: Shape the Kebabs
steps: Divide into equal portions and shape into flat discs (~2 cm thick).
Optional: Stuff with a tiny cube of cheese or dry fruits for luxury appeal.

instruction.title: Cook
steps: Heat a mix of ghee and oil in a heavy pan.
Shallow fry kebabs on medium heat until golden brown, crisp outside, soft inside.
Serve on a black stone or wooden platter.
Place kebabs over a mint-coriander chutney smear.
Garnish with pickled onions, microgreens, and a lemon wedge wrapped in muslin.
Drizzle a few dots of saffron-yogurt or beetroot reduction for fine-dining effect.

chefTips: Texture Balance: Don’t over-grind dal; slight graininess gives authentic bite.
Fat Choice: Ghee adds luxurious aroma—restaurants often use a 50:50 oil-ghee mix for balance.
Make Ahead: Shape kebabs and refrigerate (up to 24 hrs); fry just before serving.
Smoky Touch: For a tandoor-like flavor, briefly smoke mixture with a hot charcoal piece before shaping.
